summary: Full-stack testing of Old-School Phone Revival MVP. Backend: 22/22 pytest tests passed (menu, programs CRUD/toggle, dial routing incl. secret/voicemail/invalid/coming_soon, all 4 fortune personas via gpt-5.2, TTS base64 mp3, secret-codes & schedules CRUD). Frontend: split retro-CRT layout renders; lift-handset -> greeting + Fortune Teller/Voicemail menu; dial 1 -> persona list; dial 1 (Zoltan) -> AI fortune flow triggered; secret 666 -> Devil's Line displayed; hangup resets; Config Panel tabs (programs/secrets/schedules) all functional incl. add/toggle/delete flows and Live IVR menu preview reflects program toggle.
critical_code_review_comments: Task description referenced testids like 'program-toggle-dial-box' and 'program-key-dial-box' but actual code uses program slugs (fortune/adventure/therapy/loveline/pickup/haunted/santa). No such 'dial-box' slug exists; recommend aligning naming between spec and code. Tests were conducted against real slugs and passed.
SecretCode POST does not check for duplicate 'code' values (no unique index) -- could allow duplicates. Not blocking MVP.
server.py: on-startup seed uses $setOnInsert only, so edits to seed_data.py after first seed will NOT update existing docs; acceptable but worth documenting.
server.py returns program 'coming_soon' before checking secret codes only after program match failed -- ordering is fine, verified.
Frontend PhoneSimulator ~350 lines; still within acceptable range. Consider extracting call-state machine if it grows.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 7 programs & 9 secret codes on startup via /app/backend/seed_data.py. Test-created data prefixed with TEST_ and cleaned up in-flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py in /app/backend/tests covers all endpoints. Frontend testids for programs use slug (e.g. program-toggle-fortune, program-key-adventure), not 'dial-box'. Simulate scheduled call uses 9s ring timeout; not exercised in this run. Audio playback in headless is silent but base64 mp3 is confirmed returned by /api/tts.
